cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: We want to stay near your old-town place this Saturday so we can walk to dinner and back. Do you have a two-bedroom or projector room for 4?
context_window: user already sounds close to booking
location and room layout both matter

mustInclude: acknowledge the easy citywalk intention
anchor the reply in old-town access plus the 4-person room-fit need
guide toward the exact property or stay details without overselling
mustAvoid: promising availability without live context
dumping every room option

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: My train gets in around 11:20 tonight. If I book now, can I self check in without trouble?

mustInclude: acknowledge the late-arrival stress
state that access handling depends on the property and booking flow
guide toward the exact property, arrival window, and booking status
mustAvoid: guaranteeing a smooth midnight check-in without verification
pushing the user to pay first and ask later

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: We're 4 friends going to the concert next Friday. Is your place near the arena, and can we get back late without bothering anyone?

mustInclude: recognize the late-return event context
ask for the venue or exact property plus headcount or return-time details
keep boundaries around access and quiet return calm and natural
mustAvoid: encouraging late-night noise
treating every building as equally event-friendly

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: Two adults, our 6-year-old, and two big suitcases. Is the loft room okay, and do you have an elevator?
context_window: stairs and luggage may affect the stay

mustInclude: acknowledge the family-fit and arrival-access concern
ask about the bedding need while noting that loft layout and elevator access matter
guide toward room-fit clarification instead of a broad yes
mustAvoid: guaranteeing the loft works for every family
skipping the luggage or access issue

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: I need somewhere for 5 nights near the hospital for my mom's follow-up visit. Do you have a washer and a simple kitchen?

mustInclude: acknowledge the practical family reason for the stay
mention that washer and kitchen details depend on the exact room or listing
guide toward the area, dates, and the feature that matters most
mustAvoid: turning the stay into a leisure-travel pitch
saying every room has the same amenities

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: We're bringing a corgi for two nights. Can we stay in the two-bedroom room, and are pets okay?

mustInclude: acknowledge the desire to avoid pet-related friction
ask for the exact property or room plus the pet-rule context if needed
guide toward policy confirmation without oversimplifying
mustAvoid: saying all rooms allow pets
skipping pet size or building-rule context

cases.label: edge_case

input.user_message: The loft in your videos looks huge. Is it actually like that, or is it just a wide-angle camera?

mustInclude: de-escalation
invite one concrete room detail such as loft space, window, or projector setup
leave room for the user to keep the conversation going
mustAvoid: bragging about the listing
guaranteeing an exact match to video content

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: I booked through your link and now you're saying there's a cleaning fee and deposit. If I cancel, can you refund everything?

mustInclude: acknowledge the frustration around extra fees
state that refund handling depends on the booking channel and order terms
guide toward the reservation or fee details for the next step
mustAvoid: promising a full refund before checking the booking context
treating every booking source as identical

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: If I stay Friday night, can you guarantee early check-in at noon and late checkout on Saturday?

mustInclude: acknowledge the schedule concern
explain that early check-in and late checkout depend on room status or property handling
guide toward the property and stay-date details
mustAvoid: promise-like wording
acting as if timing flexibility is automatic

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: The door code didn't work, the hall smelled, and it's way noisier than the listing looked.

mustInclude: empathy first
request the property and room or booking details through a safer channel
handoff toward a human service path
mustAvoid: blaming the guest publicly
arguing about the listing in public

cases.label: happy_path

input.user_message: Send me your WeChat or phone. If the two-bedroom is free, I want to book it now.

mustInclude: welcome the intent to move quickly
use platform DM as the safe bridge
request the property or stay details as the next action
mustAvoid: posting off-platform contact publicly
sounding evasive or robotic

cases.label: failure_case

input.user_message: We're 6 people, but only 4 will sleep there. Can we still all hang out and make some noise late?
context_window: building and community risk is visible
user may still be convertible if handled calmly

mustInclude: keep the tone calm rather than confrontational
make clear that occupancy and quiet-hour rules need to be respected
guide toward confirming the actual guest count and room or property fit
mustAvoid: saying that extra visitors and late noise are fine
shaming the user
